steps.canonical_text: Check FAST: ask them to smile (face droop), raise both arms (one drifts down), and repeat a short sentence (slurred or wrong words).
Note the exact time the symptoms started, or the last time they were seen normal. This time decides treatment options.
must_keep_keywords: last time they were seen normal

steps.canonical_text: Call emergency services now even if symptoms seem to improve. Every minute matters in stroke.
Help them rest. If they are alert, sit them up with head and shoulders supported. If drowsy, lay them on their side.
Do not give food, drink, or any medication, including aspirin. Swallowing may be impaired.
Stay with them. Watch breathing and alertness. Be ready to start chest compressions if they stop breathing.
